question: What did we rule out when debugging the Stripe webhook 400 errors?
turns: Stripe is retrying payment_intent.succeeded webhooks and logging 400 responses from our endpoint. First suspicion: our webhook signature validation is broken — maybe a body parser is consuming the raw body before we can verify the signature.

Confirmed we read body as raw Buffer before any middleware runs. Ran replayed events from the Stripe dashboard — signature validation passes every time locally and in staging. Signature validation is not the issue, ruling it out.
Thought we might return 400 on duplicate events — maybe our idempotency guard has a bug. Checked the code: duplicate detection short-circuits and returns 200 immediately. Not the cause.

Root cause: our handler synchronously calls a downstream inventory service that takes 35–40 seconds to respond. Stripe requires a 200 within 30 seconds or marks the delivery failed. Moved the downstream call to a background job queue, return 200 immediately, and Stripe stopped retrying.
